key_insight: The paper proposes a fully model-free deep learning framework for high-dimensional fully nonlinear parabolic PDEs that estimates gradients and Hessians using zeroth-order simulation-based targets instead of automatic differentiation.
engineering_relevance: This enables practical PDE solving in black-box scientific and reinforcement-learning settings where the governing dynamics are unknown and only simulator queries or pointwise evaluations are available.
method: Uses two simulator types as data-generating mechanisms and a represent-then-learn strategy.
Constructs zeroth-order derivative estimators from perturbed Monte Carlo trajectories to generate training targets for gradient and Hessian networks.
Derives non-asymptotic error bounds with discretization, approximation, statistical, and ZOD-bias terms under a contraction assumption.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a statistical learning analysis and numerical experiments showing competitive performance in moderate and high dimensions, including sample complexity guarantees in weighted Sobolev space.
watch_item: Zeroth-order estimators introduce a bias-variance tradeoff, so performance can depend sensitively on perturbation design, simulator noise, and sample budget.
paper_title: A Zeroth-Order Deep Learning Method for Fully Nonlinear Parabolic Partial Differential Equations with Unknown Coefficients
